key_findings: Three root-cause failure modes require three distinct components: (1) file-list dispatch with no lease → stuck chunks (FM-1/FM-4); (2) inline sink write with no outbox → silent sink failures (FM-2); (3) no retry classification → retry storms (FM-3)

SQLite IMMEDIATE isolation level on lease grants is the minimal-change anti-duplication guarantee — two workers competing for the same chunk serialize; loser sees 0 rows updated and retries without error coordination logic

UNIQUE constraint on sink_outbox.record_id makes replay idempotent: when sweeper gets a UniqueConstraintViolation from ClickHouse/Neo4j on replay, mark SYNCED not FAILED — record already landed successfully

Authentication failure (non-retryable config fault) must HALT the sweep loop immediately — not retry — to prevent storm consuming retry budget before root cause is identified; emit alert before halting
Backpressure threshold at 10k outbox_pending_depth (reduce concurrency 25%) and 50k (halt new leasing) prevents unbounded outbox growth even when sink is degraded
Sync ledger (sink_sync_ledger table) maintained by trigger on outbox status changes provides O(1) per-chunk sink progress view without full-table aggregation at 10M+ row scale
Heartbeat-based lease extension (heartbeat every LEASE_DURATION/2) allows short lease duration for fast reclaim on crash while preventing false reclaim of actively-processing chunks
Master status query (single SQL statement) provides full pipeline observability in <100ms from one SQLite DB — no external monitoring stack required for operations

decisions.title: SQLite as single authoritative state store for both queue and outbox
decisions.summary: Chunk queue and sink outbox live in same SQLite DB; atomic cross-table transactions possible
decisions.rationale: Atomic chunk ack + outbox insert in single transaction prevents the gap where a chunk is marked COMPLETED but its records are not yet queued for sync. An external coordinator (Redis) would require a distributed commit across two systems.

decisions.title: Lease with heartbeat extension over fixed long lease
decisions.summary: Short lease (10 min) extended via periodic heartbeat; expired leases reclaimed within one reclaim cycle (5 min)
decisions.rationale: A fixed 4-hour lease to accommodate slow chunks means a crashed worker leaves a chunk unreachable for 4 hours. Heartbeat + short lease means reclaim happens within 5 min of worker death regardless of chunk size.

decisions.title: Outbox sweep in separate thread over inline sink write
decisions.summary: Scanner writes to outbox synchronously; separate sweep thread handles sink writes asynchronously
decisions.rationale: Inline sync blocks the scan worker on sink latency (up to seconds per record at ClickHouse). Sweep decouples scan throughput from sink throughput. Scan can continue at full speed while sink catches up.

decisions.title: Auth failure halts sweep with alert; no retry
decisions.summary: Non-retryable config faults (wrong credentials, schema mismatch) halt sweep loop immediately
decisions.rationale: Retrying auth failures consumes all retry budget in seconds and triggers alert spam that masks the real issue. Halt + single alert forces immediate operator investigation of the root cause.

decisions.title: Dual backpressure thresholds (10k reduce / 50k halt) over single threshold
decisions.summary: 10k pending triggers concurrency reduction; 50k pending triggers lease halt for full outbox drain
decisions.rationale: Single threshold is binary (full speed or zero). Dual threshold gives graduated response: reduce first (may be sufficient), halt only if reduction doesn't recover sink lag within the threshold band.

components.name: Sink Outbox + Sync Ledger
components.idempotency_mechanism: UNIQUE constraint on record_id; UniqueConstraintViolation → SYNCED

actions.depth_critical: halt new chunk leasing; drain outbox

integrity_slos.verification: replay completed chunk; compare row counts
inject stale lease; verify reclaim within 5 min
inject bad credentials; verify sweep halts

risk_model.title: SQLite WAL writer contention at high ingest rate
risk_model.mitigation: WAL mode; batch writes per chunk; PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL

risk_model.title: Sweep falls behind; outbox grows unboundedly
risk_model.mitigation: Backpressure halts leasing at depth >50k; sweep batches 1000/cycle

risk_model.title: Auth failure causes retry storm
risk_model.mitigation: Auth failures halt sweep immediately; no retry; single alert

risk_model.title: Lease too short for large chunks
risk_model.mitigation: Heartbeat every LEASE_DURATION/2; adjustable per chunk size profile

risk_model.title: Outbox exceeds SQLite scalability
risk_model.mitigation: Archive SYNCED records older than 7 days to sink_outbox_archive

risk_model.title: Scanner restart loses heartbeat state
risk_model.mitigation: Heartbeats resume on restart; reclaimer handles stale within 5 min

recommended_actions.action: Enable SQLite WAL mode: PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL — required before any concurrent writes
Initialize chunk_queue table with 51 chunk rows from existing file list (one-time migration)
Implement grant_lease() with IMMEDIATE isolation in persistence_gateway.py — eliminates duplicate chunk execution
Create sink_outbox table and implement outbox insert in persistence_gateway.py before any sink write
Implement outbox sweep as background thread with 30s interval and retry classification table
Wire backpressure signals to scanner concurrency controller: depth >10k → reduce 25%; >50k → halt leasing
Run SLO-1 drill (restart mid-chunk) before promoting to production — must show 0 duplicate rows
Deploy master status query as hourly cron output to ops channel — immediate pipeline observability

assumptions: A1: SQLite WAL mode will be enabled before first write
A2: persistence_gateway.py can be extended to accept chunk_id and worker_id for lease integration
A3: Scanner concurrency is controlled by a configurable pool size parameter
A4: 51 chunks pre-populated in chunk_queue at initialization
A5: ClickHouse/Neo4j clients raise distinct exception classes for retryable vs non-retryable errors
A6: audit log table (chunk_queue_audit_log) maintained by trigger for SLO-1 verification
